ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn provides form, table, radio, and dialog primitives but no ordinary billing workflow that combines plan selection, invoice semantics, payment-provider boundaries, impact preview, and destructive cancellation review.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: The kit combines controlled or uncontrolled native plan serialization, scoped invoice records, consumer-rendered provider fields, consumer-calculated change impact, and acknowledgement-gated cancellation without bundling payment integration or secrets.

visualSignature.summary: The billing ledger uses literal white, crisp Ink rows, strong hierarchy, a Green header seam, Violet selection/focus, restrained corners, scoped overflow, touch targets, and preference fallbacks shared with Mergora.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A consumer renderer explains authoritative effective-date, credit, tax, or invoice consequences before the plan request is submitted.

disabledBehavior.ui: No impact output or preview surface is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Native plan selection, serialization, reset, and submission remain unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: The preview renderer is never invoked.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No output or impact description enters the accessibility tree.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A clearly labelled shell accepts consumer or provider-hosted payment fields without collecting or transmitting card data itself.

disabledBehavior.ui: No payment-method section, provider boundary copy, or supplied fields are rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Plan and invoice behavior remains unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No supplied payment-field handlers or provider code is mounted.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No payment section, heading, fields, or descriptions enter the accessibility tree.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumer-owned per-invoice actions are available without converting invoice identifiers into unsafe guessed links.

disabledBehavior.ui: No action column or invoice action buttons are rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The complete invoice table remains readable.
disabledBehavior.events: No invoice action callback can fire.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No action header or per-row focus stops enter the accessibility tree.

optionalEnhancements.summary: An inline review requires visible consequences and explicit acknowledgement before a consumer-owned cancellation request.

disabledBehavior.ui: No cancellation section, review control, acknowledgement, or destructive action is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Plan, invoice, and payment-method behavior remains unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No cancellation state or onConfirm callback exists.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No destructive heading, consequence list, checkbox, status, or buttons enter the accessibility tree.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit axe coverage includes native plan forms, instance-scoped labelled regions, invoice tables, enhancement removal, read-only reset cancellation, acknowledgement, narrow RTL, reduced motion, forced colors, and target sizing; complete Risk Class 3 manual and financial review evidence is not recorded.

interactionEvidence.summary: Focused Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit evidence covers enhancement removal, mutable and read-only native reset semantics, repeated-instance ARIA ownership, controlled and uncontrolled plan ownership, invoice actions, cancellation acknowledgement, RTL, reduced motion, forced colors, and narrow/touch presentation; authoritative finance, provider, lifecycle, and failure matrices remain open.

parityEvidence.summary: Package output is intentionally not planned by the catalog. Deterministic generation keeps canonical source, native registry, and Shadcn payloads on the same consumer transform, while the exact-tarball Next.js and Vite record verifies source mode and the source-only package boundary.

blockers.summary: Dependency closure, financial/payment/security/privacy/legal review, large invoice and provider matrices, current manual assistive technology, public dogfooding, and Passport approval remain open.

maturityAssessment.rationale: The source workflow and focused evidence exist, but distribution, lifecycle, provider, financial, security, privacy, legal, browser, and manual assistive-technology gates remain incomplete.
